context.goal: restore minimum data needed for fair roadmap evaluation without changing strategy logic

initial_state_before_recovery.summary: Fresh migrate had effectively wiped runtime and historical data from the working MySQL database.

recovery_actions.effect: restored 10 core stocks, seeded demo news, seeded 30 daily price rows per ticker, restored watchlists and sources
all 10 core tickers now have 87 daily price rows in DB
raw 37, saved 28, updated 9, final OJK article count 43, backfill status ready
issuer news fetchers ran but mostly returned empty results or provider errors; no meaningful coverage lift for the lowest-coverage tickers
exported 10 ticker CSV files with 87 rows each
phase_a_closeout_status.json now shows closed_with_notes and runtime_ok

recovery_actions.command: python3 -m quant.finalize_baseline_v2_decision --output-dir output && python3 -m quant.audit_project_roadmap --output-dir output
recovery_actions.effect: runtime-blocked project artifacts were refreshed to reflect current MySQL and closeout state

coverage_summary.main_bottleneck: stock-linked sentiment coverage remains too thin and uneven across the 10 core tickers

provider_findings.ojk_backfill: working via fallback/paginated listing even though the RSS endpoint returns 404
provider_findings.gnews: mixed results; often returned 0 articles and also hit 429 rate limit
provider_findings.newsapi: mostly empty results for low-coverage Indonesian issuers and later hit 429 rate limit
provider_findings.finnhub: 403 access denied for issuer news requests
provider_findings.gdelt: rate-limited and timed out on some targeted recovery attempts
provider_findings.implication: price recovery is reliable, macro news recovery is reliable, but issuer-news recovery is provider-limited and inconsistent

data_quality_findings.implication: the issue is not missing scoring; the issue is sparse and noisy issuer-news retrieval

phase_a_baseline.reason: MySQL connectivity, OJK macro backfill, and daily price history have been restored; runtime artifacts now show closed_with_notes and runtime_ok

phase_b_item_5_volume_confirmation.reason: this experiment depends mainly on price and volume, and those are now available across all 10 core tickers with 87 daily rows each

phase_b_item_6_multi_timeframe.reason: daily history is restored, so the experiment is no longer blocked by empty runtime data; however the horizon is still modest and weekly retention remains a design problem

phase_b_item_7_sentiment_momentum.reason: only 2 of 10 core tickers have at least 5 sentiment days, so any 3-day momentum gate remains highly fragile even after recovery

phase_b_item_8_adaptive_per_ticker.reason: per-ticker sentiment and event coverage remain too uneven for a robust adaptive search space, even though daily prices are restored

failed_primarily_due_to_missing_or_insuf: sentiment momentum when evaluated as a per-ticker sentiment gate across all 10 core tickers
adaptive per-ticker search when it expects broader ticker-level sentiment/event support
failed_primarily_due_to_signal_or_filter: most of the baseline-v2/v4 trade-quality problem after price and macro data recovery

bottom_line.fair_reassessment_answer: yes, fresh migrate did materially distort roadmap fairness because it removed all runtime and historical data, but after recovery the main remaining blocker is not empty runtime data anymore; it is sparse, uneven, and sometimes noisy issuer-news coverage combined with baseline trade-quality limits
bottom_line.most_pragmatic_next_action: freeze this recovered dataset as the new minimum fair runtime baseline, do not reopen Phase C, do not reopen items 5-8 globally, and continue baseline redesign work with explicit awareness that price coverage is now sufficient but issuer sentiment coverage is still the limiting factor
